explanation: Single or continuous bubbles. Water boiling on a stove
fish tank bubbles.

discriminators: A bubble's cavity resonance RISES as the bubble shrinks and collapses: strongly positive pitch_slope_semitones_per_second on each micro-event. This upward chirp is the single most distinctive feature in the whole WATER category and separates BUBBLES from every noise-bed sibling.
Pitched micro-transients: harmonicity is far above FLOW, SURF, STEAM or SPRAY, which are unpitched noise.
Sparser and far more pitched than FIZZ, which is a dense unpitched high-frequency crackle with no coherent f0.
Higher f0 and faster events than LIQUID & MUD-BUBBLES, whose viscous, larger bubbles resonate lower and gloop more slowly.
Gurgling with a downward drain swirl and pulled-in air belongs in WATER-DRAIN.

explanation: Draining gurgling water
pulling in air with it as it drains
think a kitchen sink drain
or a storm drain
shower drain.

discriminators: Gurgle = bubbles embedded in a flow, pulling in air: intermediate between WATER-BUBBLES (pitched, sparse) and WATER-FLOW (stationary noise). Neither pure enough to win on its own axis.
Confined by a pipe, so more mid-band-dominant and more band-limited than open-air WATER-FLOW.
The distinction from WATER-PLUMBING is largely contextual: a drain is the gurgling suck, plumbing is the running fixture. Require filename evidence.
Less pitched and more continuous than WATER-BUBBLES; less stationary and lower-flatness than WATER-FLOW.

explanation: Water dripping
slow to fast. A cave drip
a leaking pipe dripping or a kitchen faucet dripping.

discriminators: Isolated sparse transients on a near-silent bed: the lowest onset_rate_per_second and the highest crest_factor of any WATER subcategory. Textbook impulsive morphology.
The opposite pole from WATER-FIZZ, which is the same droplet physics at 50x the density (texture, low crest).
Far smaller and quieter than WATER-SPLASH or WATER-IMPACT: a plip, not a body of water displaced. Low root_mean_square_level, tiny attack, negligible low_band_energy.
A single pitched plop (Minnaert resonance of the entrained bubble) gives moderate harmonicity, unlike a broadband splash.
If the drips densify into a continuous patter on a surface, it is RAIN, not WATER-DRIP.

explanation: That sound of Alka-Seltzer in a cup
air fizzing in water. Carbonated beverages.

discriminators: Dense high-frequency crackle: the highest onset_rate_per_second in WATER, with individual events too small and too fast to resolve a pitch. Effervescence, not bubbling.
Against WATER-BUBBLES: FIZZ has no coherent rising f0 chirp and near-zero harmonicity; BUBBLES are sparse, pitched and rise in pitch.
Against WATER-STEAM: STEAM is a smooth stationary hiss (very low spectral_flux, low onset_rate); FIZZ is granular, with resolvable micro-transients.
Against WATER-DRIP: same droplet-scale physics, opposite density and crest_factor.

explanation: General water flowing
in our case as in a river
creek or stream. Steady water. Rapids would fall under WATER-TURBULENCE
and WATERFALL has its own subcategory

discriminators: Steady water: the stationary-broadband archetype. High stationarity, LOW spectral_flux, high spectral_flatness, long. A creek or river bed that does not change.
Against WATER-TURBULENT: flux is the separator. Both are continuous and energetic; TURBULENT churns (high spectral_flux, high root_mean_square_level), FLOW does not. UCS: rapids go to TURBULENCE.
Against WATER-WATERFALL: a waterfall is far louder with much more low_band_energy rumble; UCS gives it its own subcategory.
Against WATER-SURF: SURF has a slow swell — periodic energy and centroid modulation (high spectral_centroid_deviation_hz, low onset_periodicity but visible cycles); FLOW is flat forever.

explanation: Water features and fountains
trickles or flows for a fountain
splashy fountains. From tiny to large city fountains.

discriminators: Physically a trickle plus a splash bed: a stationary noise floor sprinkled with resolvable droplet impacts. Sits between WATER-FLOW (no events) and WATER-SPLASH (nothing but events), and wins on neither axis.

A fountain is defined by being a man-made water feature, not by its waveform. Signal can only narrow it to 'small continuous water with splashes'; the filename must commit it. Do not assert FOUNTAIN over FLOW from DSP alone.
Brighter and more transient-rich than WATER-FLOW; quieter and far less low_band_energy than WATER-WATERFALL.

explanation: Percussive water impact
large crash or slam of water
as in huge wave hitting an object. Bigger and sharper than a SPLASH

discriminators: UCS, verbatim: 'Bigger and sharper than a SPLASH.' Encoded numerically — IMPACT carries roughly double the crest_factor (14 vs 8), a 5x faster envelope_attack_seconds (4 ms vs 20 ms), nearly double the root_mean_square_level (0.28 vs 0.15), and far more low_band_energy (0.35 vs 0.20, a large mass of water displaced) than WATER-SPLASH.
BIGGER: high root_mean_square_level / lufs, low-frequency-weighted, a wave slamming an object. SHARPER: fast attack, high crest, front-loaded envelope (low envelope_temporal_centroid).
A splash is a body entering water (bright, spray-rich, softer onset). An impact is water hitting a solid (percussive, low, hard onset).
Against WATER-TURBULENT: IMPACT is one discrete event; TURBULENT is a continuous churning bed.
Against LIQUID & MUD-IMPACT: mud splats are dull and damped (low spectral_centroid_hz, near-zero ring); a water impact still throws bright spray.

explanation: Water splashing against something like against the side of a boat or canoe
or a dock. Also meant for very small waves lapping on shore
like gentle very small waves at night.

discriminators: Gentle, quiet, quasi-periodic soft slaps against a hull or shore: the lowest root_mean_square_level of any WATER event subcategory. Level is the primary discriminator.
Against WATER-WAVE: a wave is a large, loud, crescendo-and-break event; a lap is a tiny ripple slap. UCS explicitly routes tiny waves here and distinct waves to WAVE.
Against WATER-SURF: SURF is the continuous unresolved roar of many waves; LAP has discrete, countable, well-separated onsets on near-silence.
Against WATER-SPLASH: much quieter, repeated rather than singular, and much softer attack.

explanation: Water sounds not fitting another category in this list.

discriminators: Catch-all. Deliberately uninformative: no gates, no priors, so it scores a neutral likelihood and becomes the abstention target when no WATER sibling wins cleanly (spec section 6).
Never assert MISC from signal evidence; it is only ever reached by abstention or by explicit filename text.

explanation: Movement in water but not underwater (see WATER-UNDERWATER) Drags through water
swimming movements
sweeps of objects through water. Thrashing in water.

discriminators: Irregular, non-stationary sloshing driven by a body or object moving through water: neither a clean single transient nor a steady bed. High spectral_flux with LOW stationarity is the signature.
UCS is explicit: this is movement in water but NOT underwater. If the spectrum is low-passed (band_limit_high_hz collapsed, high_band_energy near zero), it is WATER-UNDERWATER instead.
Against WATER-TURBULENT: TURBULENT is louder, more continuous, and more stationary — a raging river, not a swimmer.
Against WATER-SPLASH: SPLASH is one discrete entry event; MOVEMENT is a run of sustained thrashing.

explanation: Household plumbing
like a sink running
a toilet flush
the shower running. DRAIN has its own subcategory.

discriminators: A running sink, a shower and a toilet flush are acoustically just WATER-FLOW, WATER-SPRAY and WATER-DRAIN heard in a small tiled room. Nothing in the signal says 'plumbing' — the fixture is context, not physics. Text must decide.
Priors are deliberately weak and wide: mono-ish, indoor, band-limited, continuous. Their job is to prune impulses and outdoor beds, not to assert.
UCS carves DRAIN out of PLUMBING explicitly; a gurgling suck goes to WATER-DRAIN even in a kitchen sink.

explanation: Pouring water
onto ground
into a cup. Overlap with FOOD & DRINK-POUR.

discriminators: A pour has a beginning and an end: a bounded, few-second run of water noise, unlike the open-ended beds of WATER-FLOW or WATER-SURF. Duration is a real discriminator here.

Pouring INTO a vessel raises the air-column resonance as the vessel fills: mildly positive pitch_slope_semitones_per_second and elevated harmonicity. Pouring onto ground has neither, so this prior carries only moderate weight.
Against WATER-SPLASH: a pour is sustained; a splash is a transient.
Overlaps FOOD & DRINK-POUR by UCS's own admission — that boundary is semantic (container and context), never signal.

explanation: Water splashes of all kind
a person diving into a pool
a whale cresting
throwing a rock into water.

discriminators: The counterpart to WATER-IMPACT's rule 'Bigger and sharper than a SPLASH': a SPLASH is SMALLER and SOFTER. Roughly half the crest_factor (8 vs 14), a 5x slower envelope_attack_seconds (20 ms vs 4 ms), half the root_mean_square_level, and much less low_band_energy.
A splash is bright: a body entering water throws spray, so high_band_energy and spectral_centroid_hz run ABOVE those of the low, thumping WATER-IMPACT.
Against WATER-DRIP: a drip is a single tiny plip with far higher crest on far lower energy; a splash displaces a real volume of water.
Against WATER-MOVEMENT: SPLASH is one discrete entry; MOVEMENT is a sustained thrash.

explanation: Big sprays of a wave crash
to a hose spray.

discriminators: Atomised water: a high-frequency-shelved noise band, brighter and flatter than any other WATER subcategory except STEAM.

Against WATER-STEAM: nearly the same LTAS. The separators are that steam is hotter, smoother and even more stationary (lower spectral_flux, near-zero onset_rate_per_second), while a hose spray still carries droplet impacts and a sputtering nozzle. Weak axis — expect to need the filename.
Against WATER-FLOW: SPRAY is far brighter (centroid ~4 kHz vs ~2 kHz) and has less low_band_energy.
A wave-crash spray is the tail of the crash; if the transient dominates, it is WATER-IMPACT or WATER-WAVE.

explanation: Very wet hisses
radiators
saunas. Hot wet steam.

discriminators: UCS: 'very wet hisses'. A high-frequency-shelved sustained noise — the highest spectral_flatness and highest spectral_centroid_hz in WATER, with the lowest spectral_flux. Smooth, not granular.
Against WATER-FIZZ: FIZZ resolves into thousands of micro-transients (very high onset_rate_per_second, granular); STEAM has essentially no onsets at all.
Against WATER-SPRAY: same brightness family; STEAM is smoother and more stationary, SPRAY sputters. Genuinely hard — lean on text.

Against AIR-HISS: acoustically almost identical. 'Wet' steam carries slightly more low-frequency body and gurgle from condensate, but this is not a reliable DSP call. The word 'steam', 'sauna' or 'radiator' in the name is what commits it.

explanation: The general roar of the ocean
not the sport. Constant ocean roaring waves.

discriminators: UCS: 'constant ocean roaring waves'. Long, stationary, broadband, LOW spectral_flux — the sustained_noise archetype, with a slow swell that shows up as elevated spectral_centroid_deviation_hz and wide stereo_width.

Against WATER-WAVE: UCS routes the general roar HERE and distinct, individually audible waves to WAVE. The test is whether onsets resolve: SURF has none (high stationarity), WAVE has countable crescendo-break events.

Against WATER-FLOW: same low-flux stationary bed, but SURF is wider, lower (more low_band_energy rumble) and cyclically modulated; FLOW is flat and narrow. Genuinely close — the swell modulation is the axis.
Against WATER-TURBULENT: TURBULENT churns (high spectral_flux); SURF roars steadily.

explanation: Very big splashy water. Whirlpools
big rapids on a river. Dangerous water.

discriminators: The flux axis is the whole separation from WATER-FLOW. Both are continuous and energetic; TURBULENT has HIGH energy AND HIGH spectral_flux (a churning, ever-changing spectrum), FLOW has high energy and LOW flux (a static one). UCS assigns rapids here and steady creeks to FLOW.
Also louder than FLOW: high root_mean_square_level and lufs. 'Dangerous water' is loud water.
Against WATER-WATERFALL: a waterfall is even louder and more low-frequency-dominated but paradoxically MORE stationary — a constant plunge rather than a chaotic roil.
Against WATER-MOVEMENT: MOVEMENT is quieter and driven by an object; TURBULENT is the water itself.
Against WATER-IMPACT: continuous bed, not one discrete transient.

explanation: Specifically underwater sounds
flows
current and movement
Swimming underwater. See also AMBIENCE-UNDERWATER and WATER-MOVEMENT as well.

discriminators: A hard low-pass signature. Water's impedance and the air/water boundary kill everything above a few kHz, so band_limit_high_hz collapses and low_band_energy dominates. This is one of the most reliably detectable subcategories in all of UCS — a hydrophone recording cannot be mistaken for an air recording.
Against WATER-MOVEMENT: UCS explicitly splits them — same gestures, but MOVEMENT is recorded in air and keeps its high frequencies. The band_limit_high_hz gate is the entire test.
Bubble and flow content may be present; the filter, not the morphology, is what defines this class.
Shares the low-pass signature with VOICES-FUTZED and VEHICLES-INTERIOR — those are separated by voicing_ratio and by engine periodicity, not by bandwidth.

explanation: Waterfalls from small cascading ones to Niagara falls.

discriminators: A waterfall is WATER-FLOW turned up: same stationary, low-flux broadband bed, but far louder (high root_mean_square_level, lufs near -12) with a heavy low-frequency plunge rumble (low_band_energy ~0.35) and the lowest crest_factor in the category — a continuous wall with no dynamic range at all.
Against WATER-TURBULENT: rapids churn (high spectral_flux, moderate stationarity); a falls is a constant roar (low flux, very high stationarity), even though both are loud.
Level and low-end can be faked by proximity or by a big river, so this is narrowable, not separable: the word 'falls' or 'cascade' should confirm.
UCS keeps WATERFALL separate from FLOW explicitly, even though the physics is a continuum.

explanation: For general water waves
distinct waves. General ocean roar to go in WATER-SURF
and tiny waves to WATER-LAPPING. See also NATURAL DISASTERS-TSUNAMI.

discriminators: UCS: 'distinct waves'. The signature is the individual wave ENVELOPE — a slow swell, a break, a wash — giving a long envelope_attack_seconds (hundreds of ms, unlike any impact), a low onset_rate_per_second (a wave every 5-10 s), and quasi-periodic onsets.
Against WATER-SURF: UCS routes the general undifferentiated roar to SURF. SURF is stationary with no resolvable onsets; WAVE has countable ones. The stationarity / onset_periodicity pair is the axis.
Against WATER-LAP: UCS routes tiny waves to LAP. Level is the separator — a lap is a quiet slap on a hull, a wave is a loud break.
Against WATER-IMPACT: a wave's slow rising swell is the opposite of an impact's 4 ms attack, even when both peak loud.
